interaction_phrase: universities and companies are actively pursuing research
occurrence_sentence: Instead of just waiting for such ultimate quantum computers to emerge, universities and companies are actively pursuing research with the belief that we can get the useful results to society and industry with even current imperfect quantum computers.

QunaSys Inc. (Headquarters: Bunkyo-ku, Tokyo; CEO: Tennin Yan; hereinafter "QunaSys"), a company specializing in quantum computing software and algorithm development, announced the launch of a dedicated QSCI technical portal and an official partner program for hardware vendors, marking the next phase in the commercial expansion of its proprietary algorithm, Quantum-Selected Configuration Interaction (QSCI).

QunaSys Europe (QunaSys Denmark ApS) and QunaSys Japan (QunaSys Inc.) are participating in Q-Neko (The Nippon-Europe Quantum Koraborēshon), a major EU–Japan initiative aimed at strengthening collaboration in quantum technologies and high-performance computing (HPC).

interaction_phrase: unite businesses, researchers, and policymakers to cultivate a future-ready quantum ecosystem
occurrence_sentence: Accelerating quantum adoption requires cross-industry collaboration and shared expertise. At QunaSys, we unite businesses, researchers, and policymakers to cultivate a future-ready quantum ecosystem.
We operate QPARC , one of the largest communities focused on quantum adoption.

* Toshio, Akahoshi, Fujii et al. (Physical Review X , 2025) estimate that practical quantum advantage for the 8×8 Hubbard model may be achievable with on the order of 68,000 physical qubits.

interaction_phrase: Fujitsu and The University of Osaka
occurrence_sentence: * Fujitsu and The University of Osaka (2026) report that STAR-based approaches can target industrially relevant molecular systems, including catalysts and iron-sulfur clusters, with qubit requirements reduced by up to 80× compared to conventional FTQC estimates.

interaction_phrase: Fujitsu's roadmap targets practical quantum computation via the STAR architecture by 2030
occurrence_sentence: IBM has set a goal of demonstrating quantum advantage by the end of 2026. Fujitsu's roadmap targets practical quantum computation via the STAR architecture by 2030.

Many applications for quantum computers are being considered, including chemical calculations, machine learning, and financial simulation. However, these range from applications that may be realized within a few years to those that will take a decade or more, with varying degrees of impact expected at the moment. <https://www.bcg.com/ja-jp/publications/2018/next-decade-quantum-computing-how-play.aspx>
